content: People with a warm undertone are flattered by earth tones — camel, olive, rust, cream, terracotta and warm navy. Cool icy pastels and pure white tend to drain warm complexions.
Low personal contrast (hair, skin and eyes close in value) is best matched by tonal, muted outfits. High-contrast combinations like black and white overpower a low-contrast face.
An autumn colour season suits muted, warm, medium-depth colours: moss green, mustard, brick, teal, chocolate and off-white. Avoid stark black near the face; use deep brown or charcoal instead.
An oval face shape supports most hairstyles. Textured crops and short tapered sides read modern and add structure. Avoid heavy straight fringes that shorten the face.
Round faces benefit from height on top and shorter sides to add length and definition. Avoid wide, voluminous sides that emphasise width.
A rectangular build benefits from structured shoulders and layering to create depth. Tailored-not-tight fits and mid-rise trousers with a clean break lengthen the frame.
Fit is the single highest-impact lever in menswear. Tailoring the shoulders and hemming trousers transforms inexpensive pieces and outperforms buying more items.

Modern European business-casual centres on an unstructured blazer, fine-gauge knitwear, clean trousers and leather derbies or loafers. Brown leather reads warmer and more current than black for warm palettes.
Smart-casual bridges tailoring and relaxed pieces: an overshirt, a quality tee or knit, dark denim or chinos, and minimal leather sneakers or chelsea boots.
One considered accessory (a field watch or a quality belt) beats several. Match metals and leathers to the overall palette; avoid flashy logos for a refined look.
When adapting to a new country, observe the local baseline formality and palette. Northern European cities skew muted and minimal; Mediterranean climates allow lighter fabrics and warmer tones.
In a temperate climate, build around mid-weight layers — overshirts, merino knitwear and an unstructured blazer — that work across seasons and travel well.
